lessons.title: Land and Water
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Understanding the major features of Earth's surface: land and water.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Earth's surface is made up of land and water. About seventy percent of Earth's surface is covered by water, mainly in oceans. The remaining thirty percent is land. In this lesson, you will learn about Earth's land and water features.

## Main Content

**Land Features**

Earth's land includes mountains, hills, plains, valleys, and deserts. Mountains are the highest land features. Plains are flat areas. Valleys are low areas between mountains. Deserts are dry areas with very little rain.

blocks.content: Earth's surface is made up of land and water. About seventy percent of Earth's surface is covered by water, mainly in oceans. The remaining thirty percent is land.

terms.definition: The outside part of the Earth
A very tall natural feature of land
A very large body of salt water
A low area of land between hills or mountains
A large flat area of land

lessons.titleThai: แผ่นดินและน้ำ

lessons.title: Rocks and Soil
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Understanding the composition and types of rocks and soil.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

The ground beneath our feet is made of rocks and soil. Rocks come in many sizes, shapes, and colors. Soil is made from tiny pieces of broken rock mixed with living things. In this lesson, you will learn about rocks and soil.

## Main Content

**Types of Rocks**

There are three main types of rocks: igneous rocks formed from cooled lava, sedimentary rocks formed from layers of sediment, and metamorphic rocks changed by heat and pressure. Soil is made from weathered rock pieces mixed with decaying plants and animals.

blocks.content: The ground beneath our feet is made of rocks and soil. Rocks come in many sizes, shapes, and colors. Soil is made from tiny pieces of broken rock mixed with living things.

terms.definition: A solid natural material that makes up Earth's surface
The top layer of Earth's surface where plants grow
Tiny pieces of rock, sand, and mud
The breaking down of rocks by wind, water, and ice

lessons.title: Weather Patterns
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Observing and recording weather patterns.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Weather is the condition of the air outside at a particular time and place. Weather changes every day. In this lesson, you will learn about different types of weather and how to observe and record weather patterns.

## Main Content

**Types of Weather**

Sunny weather means the sky is clear and bright. Cloudy weather means the sky is covered with clouds. Rainy weather means water falls from clouds. Stormy weather includes strong wind and rain. Weather affects what we wear, what we do, and how we feel.

blocks.content: Weather is the condition of the air outside at a particular time and place. Weather changes every day. In this lesson, you will learn about different types of weather and how to observe and record weather patterns.

terms.definition: The condition of the air outside at a particular time
How hot or cold the air is

terms.thai: การพยากรณ์อากาศ
terms.definition: A prediction of future weather conditions
The usual weather conditions in an area over a long time

lessons.titleThai: รูปแบบสภาพอากาศ

lessons.title: Day and Night
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Understanding the causes of day and night through Earth's rotation.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Why does the Sun rise every morning and set every evening? Why do we have day and night? The answer lies in how Earth moves. In this lesson, you will learn what causes day and night.

## Main Content

**Earth's Rotation**

Earth spins on an axis like a top. This spinning is called rotation. It takes about twenty-four hours for Earth to complete one full rotation. The side of Earth facing the Sun has day. The side facing away from the Sun has night.

blocks.content: Why does the Sun rise every morning and set every evening? Why do we have day and night? The answer lies in how Earth moves.

terms.thai: การหมุนรอบตัวเอง
terms.definition: The spinning of Earth on its axis
An imaginary line through the center of Earth

terms.thai: พระอาทิตย์ขึ้น
terms.definition: The time when the Sun appears above the horizon

terms.thai: พระอาทิตย์ตก
terms.definition: The time when the Sun disappears below the horizon

lessons.titleThai: กลางวันและกลางคืน

lessons.title: Weather Observation
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Hands-on weather observation and recording activity.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Today you will become a weather scientist! You will observe the weather outside and record your observations using scientific tools and methods.

## Main Content

**Weather Observation Activity**

Use a thermometer to measure temperature. Observe the sky and clouds. Check the direction of the wind. Record all your observations in a weather journal. Compare your observations with the weather forecast.

blocks.content: Today you will become a weather scientist! You will observe the weather outside and record your observations using scientific tools and methods.

terms.thai: เทอร์มอมิเตอร์
terms.definition: A tool for measuring temperature
The direction the wind is blowing from
A book for recording observations

lessons.titleThai: การสังเกตสภาพอากาศ

lessons.title: Changing Earth
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - How Earth's surface changes over time through erosion, weathering, and volcanic activity.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Earth's surface is always changing. Wind, water, and ice slowly wear away rocks and shape the land. Volcanoes can create new land. In this lesson, you will learn how Earth changes over time.

## Main Content

**How Earth Changes**

Erosion is when wind and water move rocks and soil from one place to another. Weathering breaks rocks into smaller pieces. Rivers can carve valleys. Waves shape coastlines. These changes happen slowly over thousands of years, but they constantly reshape our planet.

blocks.content: Earth's surface is always changing. Wind, water, and ice slowly wear away rocks and shape the land. In this lesson, you will learn how Earth changes over time.

terms.thai: การกัดเซาะ
terms.definition: The wearing away of land by wind, water, or ice
An opening in Earth that lets out hot lava and gas
The line where land meets the ocean

lessons.titleThai: โลกที่เปลี่ยนแปลง

lessons.title: Earth Model
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Creating a model of Earth showing land and water features.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Create a 3D model of Earth! Your model should show major land features like mountains, plains, and deserts, as well as water features like oceans and rivers.

## Main Content

**Model Building**

Use clay, paint, and other materials to build your Earth model. Identify the continents and oceans. Label major landforms. Add details like rivers, lakes, and mountain ranges. Present your model and explain the features you included.

blocks.content: Create a 3D model of Earth! Your model should show major land features like mountains, plains, and deserts, as well as water features like oceans and rivers.

terms.definition: A representation of something real
One of the seven large land masses on Earth
A large natural stream of water flowing to the sea

lessons.titleThai: แบบจำลองโลก

lessons.title: Unit 6 Review
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Review activity for Earth's surface features, weather, and day/night.
lessons.content:
## Review Activity

Test your knowledge of Earth's features, weather patterns, and day and night! Identify landforms, read weather instruments, and explain Earth's rotation.

blocks.content: Test your knowledge of Earth's features, weather patterns, and day and night!

terms.definition: To recognize and name something
To make something clear by giving details

lessons.titleThai: ทบทวนหน่วย 6

lessons.title: Unit 6 Summative Assessment
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Summative assessment covering landforms, weather, and Earth's rotation.
lessons.content:
## Summative Assessment

This assessment covers everything you learned about Earth's surface, weather, and day and night in Unit 6.

blocks.content: This assessment covers everything you learned about Earth's surface, weather, and day and night in Unit 6. Good luck!

lessons.titleThai: แบบทดสอบปลายหน่วย หน่วย 6

lessons.title: Weather Week
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Recording weather observations for one week and identifying patterns.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

For this activity, you will observe and record the weather every day for one week. At the end of the week, you will look for patterns in your data.

## Main Content

**Recording Weather for a Week**

Each day, observe the sky, measure the temperature, and record the weather type. At the end of the week, create a chart showing all your observations. Look for patterns in temperature and weather conditions.

blocks.content: For this activity, you will observe and record the weather every day for one week. At the end of the week, you will look for patterns in your data.

terms.definition: Something that repeats in a predictable way
A visual display of information
Facts and numbers collected during observations

lessons.titleThai: สัปดาห์สภาพอากาศ

lessons.title: Science Journal: Earth
lessons.description: Grade 3 OUR CHANGING EARTH - Recording observations and findings about Earth in a science journal.
lessons.content:
## Introduction

Today you will use your science journal to record everything you learned about Earth's surface, weather, and day and night.

## Main Content

**Recording Earth Learning**

Draw maps showing land and water features. Record weather observations and patterns. Write down what you learned about day and night. Include any questions you still have about our changing Earth.

blocks.content: Today you will use your science journal to record everything you learned about Earth's surface, weather, and day and night.

terms.definition: A drawing of an area showing physical features
To write down information for later use

lessons.titleThai: วารสารวิทยาศาสตร์: โลก
